input.title: Pharmacy Dispensing and Inventory Control System
input.description: Pharmacy Dispensing and Inventory Control System is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Java Spring Boot, PostgreSQL for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Prescription Intake and Validation
requirements: The system must support prescription intake and validation as a complete business workflow within the application.
Prescription Intake and Validation should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with prescription intake and validation from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to prescription intake and validation for operational support.

FR-02.title: Patient Medication Profile Lookup
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end patient medication profile lookup capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Patient Medication Profile Lookup should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to patient medication profile lookup without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within patient medication profile lookup and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-03.title: Drug Interaction Alerting
requirements: The system must trigger drug interaction alerting when the relevant business events or threshold conditions occur.
Users should be able to configure delivery channels, timing, or opt-in preferences related to drug interaction alerting.
Drug Interaction Alerting must record delivery outcomes, retries, and failures for operational follow-up.
The interface should let authorized users review recent drug interaction alerting events and the current notification status.

FR-04.title: Controlled Substance Verification
requirements: Users must be able to complete controlled substance verification through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing controlled substance verification to continue.
Controlled Substance Verification must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for controlled substance verification should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-05.title: Dispensing Queue Management
requirements: The system must provide end-to-end dispensing queue management capabilities for creating, updating, viewing, and retiring relevant business records.
Dispensing Queue Management should support role-based permissions, validation rules, and duplicate-prevention checks where appropriate.
Users must be able to search, filter, and review historical activity related to dispensing queue management without leaving the module context.
The platform should maintain auditability for important actions performed within dispensing queue management and retain operational history for reporting.

FR-06.title: Barcode-based Medication Picking
requirements: The system must support barcode-based medication picking as a complete business workflow within the application.
Barcode-based Medication Picking should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with barcode-based medication picking from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to barcode-based medication picking for operational support.

FR-07.title: Batch and Expiry Tracking
requirements: The system must capture batch and expiry tracking data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Batch and Expiry Tracking should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile batch and expiry tracking records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during batch and expiry tracking.

FR-08.title: Inventory Reorder Thresholds
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete inventory reorder thresholds according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving inventory reorder thresholds changes.
Inventory Reorder Thresholds must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for inventory reorder thresholds.

FR-09.title: Supplier Purchase Order Creation
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete supplier purchase order creation according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving supplier purchase order creation changes.
Supplier Purchase Order Creation must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for supplier purchase order creation.

FR-10.title: Cold Storage Temperature Logging
requirements: The system must capture cold storage temperature logging data in real time or near real time as operational events occur.
Cold Storage Temperature Logging should provide a timeline, status history, or change log so users can understand what happened and when.
Users must be able to review, filter, and reconcile cold storage temperature logging records against the related business entities.
The platform should flag missing, inconsistent, or exception conditions that require attention during cold storage temperature logging.

FR-11.title: Prescription Refill Request Workflow
requirements: Authorized users must be able to create, update, and complete prescription refill request workflow according to the approved business workflow.
The system should validate required fields, status transitions, and role-based actions before saving prescription refill request workflow changes.
Prescription Refill Request Workflow must maintain a clear history of assignments, approvals, or status updates for operational traceability.
The interface should surface the current workflow state, next available actions, and blocking conditions for prescription refill request workflow.

requirements: The system must support insurance claim submission as a complete business workflow within the application.
Insurance Claim Submission should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with insurance claim submission from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to insurance claim submission for operational support.

FR-13.title: Cash and Card Billing
requirements: The system must support cash and card billing as a complete business workflow within the application.
Cash and Card Billing should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with cash and card billing from the relevant module.

FR-14.title: Pharmacist Verification Step
requirements: Users must be able to complete pharmacist verification step through the approved entry points provided by the platform.
The system must validate submitted credentials, identity data, and required fields before allowing pharmacist verification step to continue.
Pharmacist Verification Step must enforce secure handling of sensitive data, failed attempts, and audit logging for security review.
The user interface for pharmacist verification step should provide clear success, failure, and recovery messages for common scenarios.

FR-15.title: Rejected Claim Resolution
requirements: The system must support rejected claim resolution as a complete business workflow within the application.
Rejected Claim Resolution should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with rejected claim resolution from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to rejected claim resolution for operational support.

FR-16.title: Daily Dispensing Summary Report
requirements: The system must provide daily dispensing summary report with role-appropriate filters, date ranges, and drill-down views.
Daily Dispensing Summary Report should calculate metrics from current transactional data and keep summaries aligned with source records.
Authorized users must be able to export or share daily dispensing summary report outputs in formats used by business teams.
The platform should retain query history, report configuration, or dashboard preferences where that improves repeated analysis.

FR-17.title: Medication Recall Handling
requirements: The system must support medication recall handling as a complete business workflow within the application.
Medication Recall Handling should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with medication recall handling from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to medication recall handling for operational support.

FR-18.title: User Permission Segmentation
requirements: The system must support user permission segmentation as a complete business workflow within the application.
User Permission Segmentation should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes.
Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with user permission segmentation from the relevant module.
The platform should log significant actions, failures, and user-visible events related to user permission segmentation for operational support.

FR-01.title: Design Prescription Intake and Validation UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for prescription intake and validation. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for prescription intake and validation is complete and reviewable.
The prescription intake and validation design is responsive and accessible.
The design for prescription intake and validation aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Prescription Intake and Validation
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for prescription intake and validation. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: The system must support prescription intake and validation as a complete business workflow within the application; Prescription Intake and Validation should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes; Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with prescription intake and validation from the relevant module.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required prescription intake and validation user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for prescription intake and validation are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for prescription intake and validation is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Prescription Intake and Validation
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for prescription intake and validation. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: The system must support prescription intake and validation as a complete business workflow within the application; Prescription Intake and Validation should validate required inputs, enforce role-based permissions, and prevent invalid state changes; Users must be able to review the current state and recent activity associated with prescription intake and validation from the relevant module.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for prescription intake and validation is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for prescription intake and validation enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for prescription intake and validation is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Security Hardening for Prescription Intake and Validation
FR-01.description: Implement the security controls required for prescription intake and validation. Cover authentication, authorization, encryption, auditing, abuse prevention, and compliance checks where the SRS or workflow calls for them.
acceptance_criteria: Security controls for prescription intake and validation enforce the required authentication, authorization, or data-protection rules.
Sensitive operations and failure scenarios for prescription intake and validation are logged, validated, and protected against misuse.
The implementation of prescription intake and validation satisfies the relevant privacy, audit, or compliance expectations in the SRS.

FR-01.title: Integrate Prescription Intake and Validation with External Services
FR-01.description: Integrate prescription intake and validation with the external services or providers referenced by the SRS. Implement secure service connections, payload validation, error handling, and recovery behavior for any third-party dependency involved in the workflow.
acceptance_criteria: External or cross-system integrations required by prescription intake and validation exchange the expected payloads successfully.
Retry, timeout, and failure handling for prescription intake and validation integrations are implemented and observable.
Credentials, tokens, and outbound calls used by prescription intake and validation are configured and secured correctly.

FR-01.title: Testing Prescription Intake and Validation Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for prescription intake and validation. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for prescription intake and validation.
Security, validation, and permission checks for prescription intake and validation are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important prescription intake and validation behaviors.
